text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, leadership narrow, energy dumped hard. Record-high indices masked weak internals and heavy single-name dispersion as Mag7 strength battled broad softness and energy liquidation tied to a 4.36% USO slide and 1.49% XLE drop. Tech-related AI winners held recent gains despite Fab 7 -0.67% and Semi 7 -2.46%, while defensives modestly outperformed cyclicals with defensives_minus_cyclicals at +0.19pp in a tape still priced for growth and a neutral macro backdrop.

INDICES — VIX slipped to 16.3, below its 20d MA of 17.4. DIA led at +0.32%, then IJR +0.02%, SPY -0.02%, IWM -0.05%, RSP -0.07%, and QQQ lagged at -0.11%. STYLE shows SMALL > LARGE by 0.04pp with small +0.02% versus large -0.02%. The standout cross-asset move was USO -4.36%, confirming a sharp oil and energy unwind. No 200-day MA breach flag was triggered for SPY or QQQ based on provided data.

BREADTH — pct_above_sma20 is 52.9%, pct_above_sma50 is 59.6%, and pct_above_sma200 is 64.9%, with adv/dec at 80/126, pct_up 38.8%, and uvol/dvol at 0.53. Mag7 median 1d was +0.82% versus ex-Mag7 median -0.63%, narrow_leadership=True, SPY -0.02% versus universe median -0.60% (index strong, internals weak).

VOLUME — SPY volume was 0.9x average and QQQ volume was not listed in the index volume tranche, implying neither drew standout flow attention versus peers while price was flat-to-down, fitting a low-conviction consolidation day. XLY at 1.6x, XLF at 1.4x, and XLE at 1.3x flagged active rotations into discretionary and out of financials and energy, while sub-1.0x volume in XLB 0.9x, XLU 0.8x, and XLRE 0.7x showed those sectors being largely ignored.

SECTORS — XLY Consumer Discretionary +1.76%, XLP Staples +1.14%, XLC Comms +0.61%, XLB Materials +0.37%, XLV Healthcare +0.19%, XLI Industrials +0.00%, XLRE Real Estate -0.18%, XLK Technology -0.38%, XLU Utilities -0.42%, XLF Financials -0.83%, XLE Energy -1.49%. Top Industry ETFs were WGMI +6.12% and JETS +2.90%, while GDX -3.46% and OIH -3.98% were the weakest.

ROTATION — Money-in favored consumer and classic cyclicals via XLY +1.76% and XLB +0.37% plus leadership from The Freight 7 +1.97% and The Housing 7 +1.78%, while money-out hit financials and energy with XLF -0.83%, XLE -1.49%, The Energy 7 -1.54%, and The Bank 7 -1.15%. Defensives_minus_cyclicals at +0.19pp shows only a mild defensive lean, so the dominant state was broadening participation rather than pure flight-to-safety — label: broadening participation.

THE 7s — Top baskets were Freight +1.97%, Housing +1.78%, Old Staples +1.36%, Health +0.99%, and Mag +0.96%. Bottom baskets were Energy -1.54%, Semi -2.46%, Medtech -2.87%, and Old Metals -3.10%, with Medtech showing BSX -12.46% as the clear internal casualty beside relatively less-damaged peers.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is June 17, 2026 with CME probabilities at 100% hold, 0% cut, 0% hike and a FOMC stance tally of 6 hawks, 5 neutral, 4 doves. USD liquidity stress printed 51/100 (Elevated) with CP−Tbill spread at 0.16% in the 86th percentile as the most-stressed component, while TED spread at 0.09% and ON-RRP at $1.9B signaled no acute dollar funding seizure.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol at 1.21 and QQQ PCR_vol at 1.37 show sizable protection demand, reinforced by positive IV skew of +3.9% and +7.8%, respectively. With VIX at 16.3 below its 20d MA of 17.4 and GEX flip levels (SPY 750, QQQ 721) above current spot, dealer positioning still likely dampens modest moves but could flip more convex if indices slide toward those strikes.

CROSS-ASSET — Crude’s 4.36% USO drop and XLE -1.49% slide weighed on energy equities even as UNG +2.47% supported nat-gas-linked names, showing an internal commodity divergence. Gold -1.33% and SLV -3.18% fell alongside a flat UUP 0.00%, indicating position-driven precious metals de-risking rather than a pure dollar shock.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 61.6/100 sits in “Greed” territory. Largest single-name movers were BSX -12.46% in Medtech, SQM +4.36% in New Metals, and QCOM strength tied to an AI-chip order win from ByteDance despite broader Fab 7 -0.67%. On 20d momentum, XLK +15.91% is the best sector and Medtech -6.22% is the worst basket, while Energy +23.70% remains the best YTD basket and Software -23.20% the worst YTD basket. Vol spikes in BSX at 3.0x with price -12.46% indicate clear distribution, while XTN 2.4x with price +1.42% points to freight accumulation.

WATCH — BSX -12.46% merits focus for potential Medtech 7 spillover and whether today’s capitulation extends into broader XLV factor unwinds. SQM +4.36% with 2.4x volume inside New Metals 7 deserves monitoring as a potential leadership handoff within metals after Old Metals 7 -3.10%. XTN +1.42% on 2.4x volume inside a strong Freight 7 +1.97% flags freight and transport as an emerging cyclical leadership pocket tied to improving growth expectations.
